sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on different grid cells to see if they toggle
# Current grid:
# Row 0: g(24-26), W(29-31), g(34-36)
# Row 1: W(24-26), g(29-31), W(34-36)
# Row 2: g(24-26), W(29-31), g(34-36)

# Try clicking on each white block to see if it toggles
test_positions = [
    (50, 30),  # top-middle white
    (55, 25),  # middle-left white
    (55, 35),  # middle-right white
    (60, 30),  # bottom-middle white
]

for row, col in test_positions:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col}])
    changed = last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)
    print(f"MOUSE({row}, {col}): board_changed={changed}")
    if changed:
        # Print grid state
        for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
            if n['color'] in ['W', 'g'] and n['pixels'] == 9:
                b = n['boundary']
                rows = [p[0] for p in b]
                cols = [p[1] for p in b]
                print(f"  {n['color']} at [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")
        break

# If none worked, try clicking on the gray bar
if not any(last_action_result.get('board_changed') for _ in [0]):
    print("\nTrying MOUSE on gray bar...")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 25}])
    print(f"  Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

# Also try clicking on the blue box (left side)
print("\nTrying MOUSE on blue box (left)...")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 14}])
print(f"  Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

# Try clicking on the green bar on the right edge
print("\nTrying MOUSE on green bar...")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 63}])
print(f"  Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
